title: Home 🏠
content:
👋 Welcome# This is Xingyu Mu (穆星宇), you can call me Terry Mu if you believe x /ɕ/ and yu /y/ (the umlaut-u, ü) are hard to pronounce; or just call me Cybercricetus🐹!
👨‍💻 About# 🎓 M.S. in Computer Science @ NYU Tandon (2025 – 2027) · GPA 4.0 / 4.0 🪄 B.S. in Computer Science @ NYU Tandon (May 2025) · GPA 3.986 / 4.0 Highest GPA in the CSE department · 🏆 Pearl Brownstein Senior Award 🔬 Preference: LLM, RL — currently into agents, RAG, and alignment (DPO / PPO / GRPO) 🌏 Based in NYC · originally from China 📫 Find me at# 📧 Email — Work: xm2204[at]nyu[dot]edu; Personal: mutong221[at]gmail[dot]com 🐙 GitHub — Cybercricetus 🔗 LinkedIn — Xingyu Mu 💼 Recently# Applied ML SWE Intern @ ByteDance (May – Aug 2025) Re-architected a personalized-LLM training pipeline into a resumable state machine, parallelized API-bound stages, added a cache-aware ChromaDB layer, and shipped a 4-judge auto-eval system scoring helpfulness / correctness / empathy / completeness. Teaching Assistant — CS-UY 3224 Operating Systems @ NYU Tandon (Sep 2024 – present) Supporting 150+ students through C, x86, concurrency, and memory management. Research Assistant @ NYU AI4ce Lab — multimodal street-data collection (MapNYC). 🚀 Things I’ve built# Lazy Terminal — a GPT-style assistant that talks to your shell via the Model Context Protocol. Type English, get multi-line scripts. Universal MCP client (HTTP + STDIO), manifest-based tool discovery, GPT / DeepSeek backends. VOC Leakage Detection Robot (NYU UGSRP) — Jetson Orin Nano + CNN (ResNet-18 / AlexNet) that sniffs out volatile organic compounds at 90–95 % accuracy. Tested in a BSL-2 lab with SGP-40 sensors. MapNYC (AI4ce Lab) — LiDAR + stereo capture rig roaming Manhattan, fused through a custom ROS pipeline into 3-D point clouds. 🛠️ Toolbox# Languages — Python · C / C++ · Rust · Java · CUDA · Verilog · JS / React · x86-64 ASM ML / AI — PyTorch · TensorFlow · Transformers · LoRA / QLoRA · DPO · PPO / GRPO · RAG · vector DBs · Mem0 · Ollama 🌱 Off the clock# Historical linguistics · gym · hiking · reading · gaming

title: Paper Review
content:
📑 Paper Review# A reading log — papers I’ve read through, in roughly the order I bumped into them.
🗂️ What’s here# Notes mostly cluster around three areas:
🤖 LLMs & agents — pretraining, RAG, post-training alignment (DPO / PPO / GRPO) 🎮 Reinforcement learning — classic RL and the LLM-flavored kind 🪄 Wildcards — anything else that catches my eye 🧭 How I write them# Each post tries to answer three questions:
What problem is the paper solving? What’s the key trick that makes it work? How efffective is the trick? P.S. These are closer to self-reading notes entries than tutorials — if I got something wrong, please poke me!
P.S. Some notes were originally written on Lark, a platform that supports a Markdown format that has some differences to the standard version. To keep the original structure, I directly render the PDF for those entries.
